_comment: W2.5 / P0.11 — Stryker mutation testing over the node harness surface (§8: eat our own dog food). Stryker injects mutants (=== -> !==, +/-, boundary flips, return-value voids, …) into the files under `mutate` and reports how many the red/p0 suite KILLS. The score feeds conformance/mutation-floor.json via `scripts/mutation.mjs --run`; the gate (L14) only reads that recorded score (a full run is minutes, the gate must stay fast). Test runner = the `command` runner: it runs the relevant red/p0 drivers directly (our tests are plain node .mjs files, not a jest/mocha suite). Narrow `mutate` set = the comparators/lints/runner/fuzz-driver/bench the harness's correctness rides on; widen as .lg ports land.
commandRunner_comment: The kill command must be ORACLE-FREE: Stryker sandboxes only the harness surface (vendor-artifacts/ is ignored), so the diffcli/exec-equivalence tests that spawn the oracle-bun binary cannot run inside the sandbox. treehash-contract.test.mjs pins treehash's full behavior with NO external binary, so it is the correct kill harness for the treehash target. When a target's contract needs the oracle, mutate it under a command that stubs the oracle or run it out-of-sandbox.
ignorePatterns_comment: Stryker sandboxes the whole tree; keep the giant read-only reality (vendor/ submodules, the Rust target/, node_modules, .git, worktrees) out of the copy — vendor/bun even ships broken-symlink test fixtures that break the copy. Only the harness surface + its RED drivers need to reach the sandbox.
